pages.full_text:
the icehouse was 6m wide & high
     built of brick.

hatch                 passage to take out ice.
to shovel [diagram of icehouse structure]
ice +
Snow          drain to ice pond

There was also a mushroom house
orchard, orangerie.
pages.route_sketch_description: Hand-drawn cross-section diagram of an icehouse structure. Labels include: 'hatch to shovel ice + Snow' on the left side of the structure, 'passage to take out ice' with an arrow pointing to the right passage, and 'drain to ice pond' at the base with a curved arrow. The sketch shows a domed/rounded chamber with a hatch entrance on the left and a horizontal passage on the right.
pages.notes: This page appears to be a continuation of notes about a visit to a historic property (likely Hanbury Hall, given the right page context). The icehouse diagram is a hand-drawn explanatory sketch rather than a route map, but has been flagged as route_sketch=true as it is a hand-drawn diagram. No handwritten page number visible on this page.

pages.full_text: Hanbury Hall parterre. 2002, NT & Nick Moss
photo_captions: Hanbury Hall parterre. 2002, NT & Nick Moss
pages.notes: Page contains a glued-in photograph of Hanbury Hall (a National Trust property, red brick manor house). The caption is written diagonally alongside the photo. 'NT' likely refers to National Trust. 'Nick Moss' may be a person who accompanied the visit or a photographer credit. The handwritten page number 50 is visible in the top right corner.
